name: Tavern Entrance Test Level
flavor_text: A spacious tavern staging hall with enough room for a full party and companions, plus a dedicated bar area before the first dungeon door.

connections.description: Heavy wooden door between tavern staging area and dungeon entrance

regions.name: Tavern Entrance
regions.description: Safe staging room before the dungeon.

rooms.name: The Gilded Tankard
rooms.description: The warm glow of candlelight fills the spacious tavern hall, flickering across rough-hewn wooden floors and casting long shadows between rows of sturdy oak tables. A polished bar dominates the far wall where Eldric, the broad-shouldered barkeep, methodically wipes down tankards and keeps a watchful eye on the room. Shelves behind him hold an impressive collection of wine bottles and spirits. Nearby, Marta the Scholar sits hunched over a table cluttered with ancient spellbooks, mystical tomes, and a faded journal, her quill scratching quietly as she cross-references a crystal-bound codex. Iron sconces line the walls, their torch rods casting steady light across the open movement lanes between tables. The air is thick with the smell of ale, bread, and hearth smoke, and the crackle of the stone fireplace provides a steady undercurrent to the low murmur of conversation.

rooms.name: Dungeon Antechamber
rooms.description: The first dungeon room beyond the tavern door.

0.label: Tavern Door
0.description: Heavy wooden door between tavern and dungeon room. When open, allows passage between areas without movement penalty.

1.label: Bar Counter A
1.description: Fixed wooden bar counter segment forming the left section of the tavern's main bar. Provides a physical barrier for drink service and can provide cover during combat.

2.label: Bar Counter B
2.description: Fixed wooden bar counter segment forming the middle section of the tavern's main bar. Provides a physical barrier for drink service and can provide cover during combat.

3.label: Bar Counter C
3.description: Fixed wooden bar counter segment forming the right section of the tavern's main bar. Provides a physical barrier for drink service and can provide cover during combat.

4.label: Round Table A
4.description: Movable round tavern table suitable for small gatherings. Can be pushed by players to rearrange the tavern space or create barriers during combat. Provides light cover when used defensively.

5.label: Long Table A
5.description: Movable long wooden table for larger groups or feasts. Can be pushed by players to rearrange the tavern space or create barriers during combat. Provides light cover when used defensively.

6.label: Long Table Center
6.description: Center extension segment for a composed long wooden table.

7.label: Long Table B
7.description: Second end-cap segment for a composed long wooden table.

8.label: Stool Stack
8.description: Stack of lightweight tavern stools. Entities can share hexes with these stools, and multiple stools can occupy the same space, allowing flexible positioning around tables. Can be kicked aside or used as improvised weapons.

9.label: Crate Stack
9.description: Stack of supply crates used for tavern storage. Blocks hex occupancy but can be pushed to create temporary barriers or clear pathways. Multiple crates can be stacked in the same location. Provides light cover when used defensively.

10.label: Stone Fireplace
10.description: Large stone fireplace providing warmth and ambient light to the tavern. This fixed environmental fixture anchors the tavern's common area and provides substantial cover during combat. Adjacent hexes may have increased fire damage risk.

11.label: Decorative Rug
11.description: Woven floor rug providing decorative warmth to the tavern. Characters can move freely across it without penalty. Purely aesthetic with no gameplay impact.

12.label: Stone Wall Corner Acute
12.description: Acute-corner stone fortress wall tile for hex-grid perimeter turns.

visual.prompt: A top-down orthographic 2D map tile of a sturdy grey stone fortress wall turning through an acute corner inside a hexagonal frame. The wall is made of heavy weathered masonry blocks with visible mortar and crenellations. The ground is a neutral stone floor texture. Clean hexagonal border, tabletop RPG style, flat lighting, high detail, white background.

13.label: Stone Wall Corner Obtuse
13.description: Obtuse-corner stone fortress wall tile for hex-grid perimeter turns.

visual.prompt: A top-down orthographic 2D map tile of a sturdy grey stone fortress wall turning through an obtuse corner inside a hexagonal frame. The wall is made of heavy weathered masonry blocks with visible mortar and crenellations. The ground is a neutral stone floor texture. Clean hexagonal border, tabletop RPG style, flat lighting, high detail, white background.

14.label: Stone Wall Flat EW
14.description: East-west oriented straight stone wall segment for hex-grid perimeter boundaries.

15.label: Stone Wall Flat NS
15.description: North-south oriented straight stone wall segment for hex-grid perimeter boundaries.
